loops.verdict_explanation: Test suite is red (2 deterministic failures) due to a stale test helper that duplicates the CLI arg contract without codex_home_manifest. Production CLI is unaffected. All structural scoring is unverifiable while the suite is red; loop 1 restores green before loop 2 can score architecture honestly.

credibility.proof: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:66-82 omits codex_home_manifest; AttributeError at run_review.py:306

strengths: Production CLI unaffected: argparse supplies None default for --codex-home-manifest; only the test helper's manually-constructed Namespace is stale.
Root cause is localized to a single dict in scripts/tests/_helpers.py (lines 66-82); minimal blast radius.

findings.title: Test suite red blocks structural review
findings.why_it_matters: Two codex test paths fail with AttributeError, making the suite red and preventing any structural scoring under the contest rubric.
findings.what_is_wrong: scripts/tests/_helpers.py make_args() (lines 66-82) hard-codes a parallel copy of the CLI arg names without codex_home_manifest. run_review.py:306 reads args.codex_home_manifest; the Namespace from make_args() omits it — AttributeError on both codex test paths.

findings.locality_impact: Changes to run_review.py's arg contract must be reflected in two places; no single-file update is complete.
findings.metric_signal: 2 of 118 tests fail deterministically; re-run confirms (not a flake).
findings.why_weakens_submission: A red test suite makes implementation credibility unverifiable and blocks all structural scoring; no dimension above 1 can be grounded while tests fail.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Add codex_home_manifest: None to the defaults dict in scripts/tests/_helpers.py make_args() after review_id.

deepening_candidates.source_friction_proven: F1 — duplicated arg contract drifted from parse_args(); AttributeError on new arg.
deepening_candidates.why_shallow_or_misplaced: make_args() is a hand-rolled dict that duplicates run_review.parse_args() authority; it cannot stay in sync automatically.
deepening_candidates.behavior_to_move_behind_interface: Derive the Namespace from parse_args([]) with controlled overrides, eliminating the separate dict.
deepening_candidates.test_surface_after_change: Tests would construct args via parse_args() + override; helper cannot drift.
deepening_candidates.smallest_first_step: Replace make_args() dict with: base = vars(parse_args([])); base.update(overrides); return argparse.Namespace(**base).
deepening_candidates.what_not_to_do: Do not keep the dict and derive from parse_args() as a parallel check — that doubles the authority, not removes it.

builder_notes.pattern: Duplicated arg contract in test helper
builder_notes.how_to_recognize: grep for argparse.Namespace(** or manually-constructed Namespace in test helpers — each is a stale-arg bomb.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Have the test helper call the actual parse_args([]) with a controlled arg list; no manual dict, no drift risk.
builder_notes.stack_example: Python: args = parse_args([]); args.reviewer = 'claude'; args.plan_file = tmp — no dict, no drift.

builder_notes.how_to_recognize: When the failing test names a single AttributeError with a single missing attribute, the fix is one line.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Add only the missing entry; commit only the changed file; defer adjacent ruff violations to a later credibility finding.

Once loop 2 runs green tests, real scoring begins. The make_args() drift pattern will surface as a credibility or test-strategy finding in loop 2.

findings.title: Test helper re-exports stdlib it does not use, generating pervasive lint noise
findings.why_it_matters: The star-import pattern forces all 7 test files to suppress ruff lint via noqa annotations, disabling F401/F405 detection that would catch future undefined-name errors.
findings.what_is_wrong: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:12-22 imports 9 stdlib modules (json, os, shutil, signal, stat, subprocess, sys, tempfile, unittest/mock) that _helpers.py does not itself use. Test files already import these directly on their own line 2. Removing them from _helpers.py breaks nothing and eliminates pervasive lint suppression.
evidence: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:12-22 (F401 unused imports: json, os, shutil, signal, stat, subprocess, sys, tempfile, unittest, mock)
scripts/tests/test_execution_paths.py:2 (imports same stdlib directly — duplicate path)
ruff F405 at test_execution_paths.py:18,31,39 (self_check may be undefined or from star imports)
ruff F405 at test_execution_paths.py:47,52,57 (run_script may be undefined or from star imports)
findings.leverage_impact: Zero: each test file already imports stdlib directly; star re-export provides no new capability.
findings.locality_impact: Negative: F401 noise in _helpers.py + noqa suppressions in 7 test files degrade ruff signal across the entire test directory.
findings.metric_signal: ruff exit code 1; 9 F401 violations in _helpers.py; 3 F405 violations in test_execution_paths.py
findings.why_weakens_submission: A test helper generating structural lint noise across 7 files and suppressing undefined-name detection is a credibility deduction; it signals accumulated drift and prevents tooling from flagging future issues.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Remove unused stdlib imports from scripts/tests/_helpers.py:12-22 (json, os, shutil, signal, stat, subprocess, sys, tempfile, unittest, mock). Keep: import argparse (used at :85), from run_review import ... (used at :32-34), from _common.* imports (used at :35-37). Each test file's direct imports on line 2 already cover stdlib needs.

findings.title: make_args() has no contract-sync test — will drift again on next arg addition
findings.why_it_matters: Loop 1 was entirely consumed by make_args() drifting from parse_args(). The fix was one dict entry; the structural cause is unchanged. Next parse_args() addition will silently break codex tests again.
findings.what_is_wrong: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:64-85 builds a Namespace by hand from a hard-coded dict. No test asserts that make_args().keys() == parse_args([]).keys(). Authority Map: Split and ambiguous — two sources of truth with no mechanical enforcement.
evidence: scripts/tests/_helpers.py:64-85 (manual dict — 16 keys today; parse_args/make_args key sets verified to match via Python introspection but no automated enforcement)
run_review.py:76-123 (parse_args() authoritative definition)
Loop 1 finding F-001 resolved by one-line dict patch with no structural prevention
findings.leverage_impact: Low: make_args() proxies parse_args() with no added behavior; a parse_args()-derived version would be identical at call sites.
findings.locality_impact: Every new CLI argument requires two edits: run_review.py:parse_args() + _helpers.py:make_args(). The second is easy to miss.
findings.metric_signal: none (drift is invisible until a test fails)
findings.why_weakens_submission: A helper that drifted once and has no mechanical prevention is a credibility risk; the backlog carries the fix rather than the certitude.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Add a test (~5 lines) in scripts/tests/test_file_io_validation.py: patch sys.argv to ['run_review.py'], call run_review.parse_args(), assert set(vars(args).keys()) == set(vars(make_args()).keys()). This prevents future drift without changing make_args() itself.

findings.title: run_review() 405-LoC flat function body with no sub-function structure

findings.why_it_matters: run_review() interleaves provider-specific environment setup, subprocess dispatch and retry, metadata/output extraction, and session persistence as a single flat function body (~411 LoC, ~67 AST control-flow nodes). Maintainers cannot navigate concerns independently.
findings.what_is_wrong: No named sub-functions or extracted module-level helpers decomposing the four concerns: env setup, subprocess dispatch, result extraction, session persistence.
evidence: run_review.py:201-612 — single function body, no helpers. Estimated ~67+ AST control-flow nodes.
Four distinct concerns interleaved: env setup (~:227-340), subprocess dispatch (~:340-465), output extraction (~:465-530), session persistence (~:530-580).
findings.leverage_impact: Low (callers invoke run_review() cleanly; internal complexity is hidden)
findings.locality_impact: Moderate (a change to agy cleanup at ~line 603 requires reading past subprocess dispatch ~340-440 and output extraction ~470-530)
findings.why_weakens_submission: A 9.5-grade function at this scale has extractable concerns named by sub-functions. Primary drag on architecture_quality and simplicity dimensions.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Extract _run_attempt(args, session_id, codex_home, gemini_config_dir, agy_log_path, logger) (subprocess setup + dispatch + capture); optionally _build_attempt_env(args, reviewer, codex_home) (env dict construction). Keep run_review() as coordinator: setup -> loop(attempt) -> extract -> persist.

findings.title: run_review() attempt-loop + codex-capture phases remain inline (irreducible without ceremony)
findings.architectural_test_failed: Shallow module (a _run_attempt() helper with 6 inputs + a 4-tuple output would have Interface approx Implementation — no Depth, only relocation; its deletion test would pass, signalling a pass-through)
findings.why_it_matters: After this loop's three extractions run_review() is 328 lines (down from ~405). The two largest remaining phases — the two-attempt resume/fresh subprocess loop and the per-run CODEX_HOME setup — are cohesive concerns a reader would prefer to grasp by name, but extracting them honestly requires threading 4-5 mutable locals both into and out of the helper.
findings.what_is_wrong: The attempt loop reads and mutates use_resume, session_id, fallback_used, and returncode across iterations, and those values are read again after the loop (session-id capture reads session_id; _build_session_data reads fallback_used). The codex-setup block mutates session_id and use_resume on its fail-closed path. Extracting either means a 4-tuple return plus 6+ parameters — ceremony that fails SPT Q2 and Q3.
evidence: run_review.py attempt loop: the resume-fallback branch sets use_resume = False; session_id = None; fallback_used = True; continue — then post-loop code at the session-id capture + _build_session_data call sites reads those same locals.
Per the contest HONESTY BAR and method.md SPT fake-clean anti-examples, a split that renames region comments into shallow helpers is fake simplification and is rejected.
findings.leverage_impact: Low (callers invoke run_review() cleanly; the residual flat region is internal)
findings.locality_impact: Moderate (the attempt-loop and codex-setup phases must still be read in the function body rather than understood from a signature)
findings.why_weakens_submission: A perfect 9-anchor would have every cohesive phase behind a named helper. The honest finding is that these two phases cannot reach that bar without ceremony, so the dimension is held at 6.5/7 rather than inflated — the contest-grade ceiling absent a costume layer.
findings.minimal_correction_path: Either accept the inline phases as the honest state (recommended — they earn placement per the deletion test), OR return a small frozen result type CodexCapture(home, capture_enabled, sessions_before, clear_session) from the codex-setup phase and have the caller apply the session_id = None; use_resume = False clear, converting one inout pair to a value return (marginal win; defer unless re-prioritized).
findings.blast_radius: scripts/run_review.py only (if pursued). Avoid: all other files, _common/**.

simplification_check.structurally_necessary: The three extractions (_setup_gemini_config, _build_stdin, _build_session_data) pass SPT Q1-Q5: each fixes real low-Locality (a cohesive phase was inlined), is the smallest honest fix (a single named helper with explicit inputs->outputs), adds no duplicate layer (module-internal privates), keeps runtime behavior identical (119 tests green, same count), and improves the product (a reader navigates by name).
simplification_check.helpful_simplification: The gemini-overlay phase (~40 lines) and the 25-field session-dict assembly are the two highest-payoff extractions; _build_stdin is small but removes an if-nest from the body.
simplification_check.should_not_be_done: Do not extract the attempt loop or codex-setup into helpers (fails the SPT honesty bar — 4-5 inout locals). Do not touch test files. Do not pursue F-006 as 'delete the dict' — it is not pure-subtractive.
simplification_check.tests_after_fix: 119 tests pass (same count). No tests added or deleted; extracted helpers covered transitively (Indirect Interface coverage carve-out).

backlog.title: Derive make_args() from parse_args([]) directly
backlog.why_it_matters: Would eliminate the two-step edit requirement. Correction to prior framing: NOT pure-subtractive — parse_args() takes no argv param and reads sys.argv; make_args() defaults reviewer='claude' whereas parse_args([]) yields reviewer=None, so the swap changes the test default and fails SPT Q4 unless every call site is updated. The loop-3 sync test already detects drift.
backlog.score_impact: test_strategy +0.5 (7.5->8) if done cleanly; lower priority given the not-pure-subtractive correction

builder_notes.how_to_recognize: A candidate helper that needs 5+ mutable locals both in and out is line-relocation, not Locality — its deletion test passes, which is the tell that it's a pass-through.
builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Extract only clean-boundary phases (explicit inputs->outputs); for the rest, record the inout-threading count as the rejection reason and leave the phase inline.

builder_notes.smallest_coding_rule: Surface uncertainty in builder notes rather than inflating the score; record marginal correction paths as deepening candidates instead of asserting impossibility.

loops.narrative: Loop 5 is the honest-partial decomposition of F-005. Three cohesive phases of run_review() — the Gemini config overlay (_setup_gemini_config), the stdin prompt prep (_build_stdin), and the 25-field session-dict assembly (_build_session_data) — are extracted into named module-level helpers, dropping the function body from ~405 to 328 lines and moving simplicity from 6 to 6.5. The decisive judgment was what NOT to extract: the two-attempt resume/fresh subprocess loop and the per-run CODEX_HOME setup thread 4-5 mutable locals (use_resume, session_id, fallback_used, returncode) both into and out of any candidate helper, so extracting them would add ceremony and fail the SPT — they are left inline with the rejection recorded, not papered over with shallow _part1()/_part2() splits. This preserves the risk-boundary invariants the directive named: _active_proc single writer, SIGTERM/SIGINT save/restore ordering, env isolation, and resume/fresh semantics all live in the un-extracted attempt loop and finally block, so the green 119-test suite is direct evidence they held. F-005 is carried forward (cheap wins taken; remainder at the honesty ceiling). F-006 is carried with a correction: it is not the 'pure subtractive dict deletion' prior loops assumed — parse_args([]) flips the test's reviewer default and needs an argv seam, so it fails SPT Q4 as specified.

loop_result.what_changed: Partially resolved F-005 in scripts/run_review.py by extracting the three genuinely self-contained phases of run_review() into named module-level private helpers: _setup_gemini_config(args, env) -> str|None (the ~40-line Gemini temp config overlay), _build_stdin(reviewer, prompt_file) -> str|None (prompt read + agy read-only preamble), and _build_session_data(args, session, meta, reviewer, new_session_id, fallback_used) -> dict (the 25-field resume-metadata session dict assembly). The run_review() body dropped from ~405 LoC to 328 LoC. The two largest phases (the two-attempt resume/fresh subprocess loop and the per-run CODEX_HOME setup) were deliberately left inline because extracting either threads 4-5 mutable locals (use_resume, session_id, fallback_used, returncode) both into and out of the helper, failing the SPT honesty bar.
loop_result.evidence_change_is_honest: python3 -m ruff check scripts/run_review.py -> All checks passed! python3 -m pytest scripts/tests/ -> 119 passed, 0 failed (unchanged count). run_review() body LoC dropped from ~405 (loop 4 commit d1bdfb4) to 328 (verified via awk span measurement). Risk-boundary preservation (Meta-Rule 4): _active_proc single writer, signal save/restore ordering, env['CODEX_HOME'] mutation, and resume/fresh semantics all remain in the un-extracted attempt loop + finally block — none crossed a helper boundary, so the invariants are preserved by construction and the green suite is the primary evidence.

implementation_review.reason: All three checks passed: Reality (ruff exits 0 post-diff; pytest 119 passed; the three helpers exist at run_review.py:202/249/265 and are called from run_review(); body LoC dropped from ~405 to 328 via awk span measurement); Honesty (no new seam — helpers are module-internal privates; the SPT downgrade on the attempt-loop/codex-setup phases is recorded with the inout-threading reason rather than forced into shallow splits; the three extractions are behavior-preserving pure relocations; _build_session_data is pure assembly; the simplicity delta cites a concrete LoC drop against d1bdfb4); Regression (extractions preserve _active_proc single-writer, signal save/restore ordering, env isolation, and resume/fresh semantics — all in the un-extracted attempt loop; 119 tests green, same count; no new findings at equal-or-higher severity).
